content_markdown: Light of intensity $I_0$ is incident on a snow-covered area of Earth. In a model of this situation, the albedo of the cloud is $0.30$ and the albedo for the snow surface is $0.80$. What is the intensity of the light at P due to the incident ray $I_0$?

diagram_metadata.description: A ray of light with intensity I_0 is incident on a snow surface. It reflects off the snow, travels upwards to a cloud, and then reflects off the cloud down to a point P.

vibe_explanation: Albedo is the ratio of reflected intensity to incident intensity. To find the intensity at P, we follow the path of the ray: first, it reflects off the snow (albedo = 0.80), so the intensity becomes $0.80 I_0$. Then, this reflected light hits the cloud and reflects again (albedo = 0.30). The final intensity at P is $0.30 \times (0.80 I_0) = 0.24 I_0$.
